events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes multiple hazards on a residential jobsite: cords across walkways, missing guardrails, blocked egress/extinguishers, improvised scaffolds, unattended open holes. Work is in-progress. This is clearly a fail with critical/high risk.

Let me construct the JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Electrical cords run across walkways creating trip and electrical hazards",
    "Missing guardrails at open edges/platforms creating fall hazards",
    "Blocked egress paths preventing safe emergency exit",
    "Blocked or inaccessible fire extinguisher reducing emergency response capability",
    "Improvised/non-engineered scaffold in use without proper fall protection or competent person inspection",
    "Unattended open floor holes without covers or guardrails creating fall-through hazards"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Relocate cords overhead or protect with rated cord covers; remove from walkways immediately",
    "Install compliant guardrails or personal fall arrest systems at all open edges before work continues",
    "Clear all egress paths of materials, tools, and debris",
    "Ensure fire extinguishers are unobstructed, visible, and accessible at all times",
    "Remove improvised scaffold; erect OSHA-compliant scaffold per manufacturer instructions with competent person inspection",
    "Cover open holes with secured covers rated for anticipated loads or guard them immediately; never leave unattended open holes"
  ],
  "rationale": "Multiple serious safety hazards are present simultaneously on an active residential jobsite, including fall hazards from missing guardrails and open holes, trip/electrical hazards from cords, blocked egress and extinguishers, and an improvised scaffold. These violate OSHA general duty and 1926 Subpart L requirements. Work must stop until controls are in place.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA General Duty Clause Section 5(a)(1)",
    "29 CFR 1926 Subpart L - Scaffolds",
    "29 CFR 1926.501 - Fall Protection",
    "29 CFR 1926.502 - Fall Protection Systems Criteria and Practices",
    "29 CFR 1910.157 - Portable Fire Extinguishers (access/egress)"
  ]
}
